title: Impeller Speed Deviation — GR-204 Granulation
description: Impeller speed dropped to 580 RPM (validated range: 600–800 RPM) during binder addition phase. Duration: ~4 minutes before self-correction.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Possible motor load fluctuation during high-viscosity binder phase. No equipment malfunction detected. Batch integrity likely maintained based on duration and magnitude.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Review granule moisture content and particle size distribution at sampling point. Consider additional in-process testing. Work order for motor load calibration check recommended as preventive measure.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Perform immediate in-process moisture check (target 8–12%). 2. Increase sampling frequency for this batch. 3. Review motor current logs for trending. 4. Schedule preventive calibration of impeller motor load cell within 30 days.
ai_analysis.regulatory_reference: GMP Annex 15 §6.3 — Process Parameter Deviations; SOP-DEV-001 §4.2

title: Inlet Air Temperature Excursion — 72°C peak
description: Inlet air temperature exceeded validated upper limit of 70°C, reaching 72°C for 6 minutes. Batch B26031201 affected during final drying phase of granulation.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Heating element control loop overshoot. Temperature sensor calibration drift identified as contributing factor.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Full thermal mapping of batch required. Moisture content testing at 5 sampling points. PSD analysis required before batch release.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Immediate: additional moisture and PSD testing. 2. Short-term: recalibrate temperature PID controller. 3. Long-term: implement dual-sensor redundancy for critical thermal parameters.

resolution.rejection_reason: Batch B26031201 rejected. Thermal excursion duration and magnitude outside acceptable limits per QA assessment. Batch quarantined and destroyed.

title: Spray Rate Below Minimum — Binder Distribution Concern
description: Binder spray rate fell to 42 g/min (validated min: 50 g/min) for 8 minutes due to peristaltic pump partial blockage.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Partial blockage of spray nozzle filter detected. CMMS maintenance log shows filter replacement was 45 days overdue.
ai_analysis.recommendation: In-process granule distribution test required. If granule uniformity within spec, batch may proceed with enhanced sampling. CMMS work order for nozzle cleaning and filter replacement required.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Granule uniformity testing at 3 sampling points. 2. Clean and replace spray nozzle filter (CMMS WO). 3. Review PM schedule for spray system — reduce filter replacement interval to 30 days.

resolution.approval_notes: Granule uniformity within spec (CV 4.2%, limit 5%). Batch released with enhanced statistical sampling of final tablets. PM schedule updated.

title: Moisture Content Out of Spec — Post-Drying Check
description: In-process moisture content measured at 4.1% against validated upper limit of 3.5% at the 2-hour drying checkpoint.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Inlet air temperature control confirmed within range. Root cause: initial batch moisture content was elevated (above 15%) entering dryer due to extended granulation time in GR-204. Higher initial moisture requiring extended drying.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Extend drying time by 30 minutes and retest moisture. If passes, batch may proceed. Review BPR for granulation moisture target and drying time correlation.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Extend drying 30 min and retest. 2. Add granulation exit moisture check to BPR as mandatory IPT. 3. Update drying time calculation algorithm to account for inlet moisture.

resolution.approval_notes: Extended drying 30 min. Retest: moisture 2.8% (within 1.0–3.5% limit). Batch released. BPR updated with corrective note.

title: Impeller Speed Deviation — Bearing Wear Detected
description: Impeller speed consistently 15–20 RPM below setpoint for entire batch. Investigation revealed bearing wear causing increased mechanical resistance.

ai_analysis.root_cause_hypothesis: Vibration analysis and CMMS motor current trending confirmed bearing deterioration. PM was 10 days overdue. Increased current draw of 12% above baseline over 3-week period prior to incident.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Immediate equipment quarantine required. Full bearing replacement before return to service. Validation reassessment of granulation parameters post-repair. Batch disposition: reject due to duration of parameter excursion.
ai_analysis.capa_suggestion: 1. Quarantine GR-204. 2. Replace impeller bearings (both primary and secondary). 3. Full IQ/OQ requalification post-repair. 4. Implement vibration monitoring trending. 5. Review PM frequency — reduce to quarterly.

resolution.rejection_reason: Batch rejected. Full-batch parameter deviation for critical process step. Equipment quarantined. Bearing replacement WO issued.
